seoTitle: Find Uncleared Checks in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to locate uncleared checks in QuickBooks Online so you can reconcile accounts accurately and avoid overdrafts or missed payments.
intro: Uncleared checks can throw off your cash flow picture if they sit unnoticed in your books. This guide walks through how to pull up a report of outstanding checks in QuickBooks Online, so you always know what money hasn't left your account yet.

steps.heading: Open the checks report
steps.body: In QuickBooks Online, head to the Reports section and search for a report that shows check activity, such as the Check Detail or a bank-related report.

steps.heading: Filter for uncleared status
steps.body: Apply a filter to show only transactions that haven't been marked as cleared, isolating the checks still outstanding against your bank balance.

steps.heading: Review the outstanding checks
steps.body: Go through the filtered list to confirm which checks are legitimately still in transit versus ones that may need follow-up, such as stale or lost checks.

steps.heading: Reconcile against your bank statement
steps.body: Cross-check the uncleared checks with your latest bank statement to make sure your books and your actual bank balance line up.

faq.question: Why does it matter if a check is uncleared?
faq.answer: An uncleared check means the funds haven't actually left your bank account yet, so your true available balance is different from what your books show until it clears.

faq.question: Can I automate tracking of uncleared checks?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online's reconciliation tools and third-party integrations can help flag outstanding checks automatically, reducing the need for manual review.

faq.question: What should I do with a check that never clears?
faq.answer: If a check remains uncleared for an unusually long time, follow up with the payee and consider voiding and reissuing it once you confirm it wasn't received or cashed.

faq.question: Is this feature available on all QuickBooks Online plans?
faq.answer: Reporting and reconciliation tools are available across QuickBooks Online plans, though the exact report layout may vary slightly by subscription tier.
